title: MARSHMALLOW ROOT
description: Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis L.) is a medicinal plant containing mucilaginous polysaccharides that form a protective film on mucous membranes. The root extract has demonstrated anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and cytoprotective effects on epithelial cells and macrophages in laboratory studies. Traditionally used for centuries to treat respiratory irritation, cough, and mild gastrointestinal discomfort.

research.title: Anti-inflammatory and Anti-oxidative Effects of Phytohustil and Root Extract of Althaea officinalis L. on Macrophages in vitro
research.summary: Marshmallow root extract protected human macrophages against oxidative stress, reduced inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, IL-8, TNF-α), and decreased ROS production while maintaining cell viability and migratory capacity.

research.title: Aqueous extracts and polysaccharides from Marshmallow roots: Cellular internalisation and stimulation of cell physiology of human epithelial cells in vitro
research.summary: Marshmallow root polysaccharides stimulated epithelial cell viability and were internalized into cells, while forming bioadhesive layers on fibroblasts, with gene expression analysis showing up-regulation of cell adhesion proteins and growth regulators.

research.title: Phytochemical Characterization of Low Molecular Weight Constituents from Marshmallow Roots and Inhibiting Effects of the Aqueous Extract on Human Hyaluronidase-1
research.summary: Marshmallow root aqueous extract demonstrated inhibitory effects on human hyaluronidase-1 enzyme, which may contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties and mucous membrane protective effects.

sentiment.summary: Marshmallow root receives limited direct discussion in user communities, with most mentions appearing in broader herbal remedy contexts. Users seeking natural anti-inflammatory and gut health solutions show interest, but specific experiential reports are scarce compared to other supplements.

effects.label: Immediate Relief:
effects.description: Provides quick soothing effect on irritated mucous membranes upon contact

effects.label: Synergistic Benefits:
effects.description: Often works better in combination with other herbal remedies for respiratory and digestive issues

effects.label: Traditional Validation:
effects.description: Long-standing use supported by modern in vitro research showing cellular mechanisms

effects.label: Targeted Action:
effects.description: Most effective for localized irritation rather than systemic inflammation

dosage.label: Tea Preparation:
dosage.description: Commonly used as herbal tea for throat irritation and cough relief

dosage.label: Age Restrictions:
dosage.description: Suitable for adults, adolescents and children over 3 years for respiratory issues; adults and adolescents over 12 for digestive complaints

dosage.label: Duration:
dosage.description: Recommended for short-term use (1 week maximum) unless symptoms persist

dosage.label: Extract Forms:
dosage.description: Available as dry extracts, liquid extracts, syrups, and raw polysaccharide preparations

sideEffects.label: Minimal Toxicity:
sideEffects.description: Generally well-tolerated with few reported adverse effects in clinical use

sideEffects.label: Ethanol Considerations:
sideEffects.description: Alcohol-based extracts restricted to adults only due to ethanol content

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Mucilage may affect absorption of other medications when taken simultaneously

sideEffects.label: Allergic Reactions:
sideEffects.description: Rare cases of allergic response in individuals sensitive to Malvaceae family plants

availability.label: Wide Availability:
availability.description: Commonly found in herbal tea blends, throat lozenges, and cough syrups

availability.label: Regulatory Status:
availability.description: Recognized by European Medicines Agency for traditional herbal medicinal use

availability.label: Combination Products:
availability.description: Frequently combined with other respiratory herbs in commercial formulations
